name: RETATRUTIDE
shortDescription: Retatrutide is a triple agonist peptide representing the cutting edge of weight loss research.
longDescription: Retatrutide is a triple agonist peptide representing the cutting edge of weight loss research. It targets GLP-1, GIP, and glucagon receptors simultaneously, showing remarkable results in clinical trials for weight reduction.
mechanisms: Triple agonist targeting GLP-1 (appetite/insulin), GIP (metabolic efficiency), and glucagon (fat burning) receptors for synergistic metabolic effects.

evidence.summary: Based on 30 human studies including 5 randomized controlled trials, retatrutide demonstrated dose-dependent weight loss of up to 24.2% at 48 weeks compared to 2.1% for placebo, with additional benefits including HbA1c reductions up to 2.02% and liver fat reductions up to 82.4% in patients with type 2 diabetes and metabolic dysfunction-associated fatty liver disease. The evidence base supports retatrutide's mechanism as a combination entero-pancreatic hormone treatment positioning it as an effective pharmacological intervention for weight management and metabolic improvement.

keyStudies.summary: Retatrutide improved HbA1c by up to 2.02% and reduced body weight by up to 16.94% in type 2 diabetes.

keyStudies.summary: Retatrutide reduced liver fat by up to 82.4% at 24 weeks, with 86% achieving normal liver fat levels.

fdaStatus.notes: Research Only Not currently approved for human use in the US. Available as a research compound. Not eligible for compounding.

longDescription: Tirzepatide is a dual GLP-1/GIP agonist that has shown exceptional weight loss results in clinical trials. Following FDA compounding restrictions similar to semaglutide, research vendor demand has surged significantly.
mechanisms: Dual incretin agonist targeting both GLP-1 and GIP receptors for enhanced metabolic effects beyond single-agonist peptides.

name: BPC-157 BPC-157
shortDescription: (Body Protection Compound-157) is one of the most popular and extensively researched peptides for tissue repair and healing.
longDescription: (Body Protection Compound-157) is one of the most popular and extensively researched peptides for tissue repair and healing.
mechanisms: Promotes angiogenesis (blood vessel formation), modulates nitric oxide synthesis, and influences growth factor expression. May support tendon, ligament, muscle, and gut tissue repair.

evidence.summary: Of 30 total studies identified, 22 involved human subjects but none were randomized controlled trials. Key findings include 87.5% of knee pain patients showing significant improvement with BPC-157 injection, consistent positive healing effects in animal models for soft tissue injuries, and accelerated tendon fibroblast outgrowth through the FAK-paxillin pathway, though the absence of RCTs limits the strength of evidence for clinical efficacy.

name: SEMAGLUTIDE
shortDescription: Semaglutide is the GLP-1 agonist that revolutionized weight loss treatment.
longDescription: Semaglutide is the GLP-1 agonist that revolutionized weight loss treatment. Following FDA resolution of shortages in 2025 and subsequent compounding restrictions, research-grade semaglutide has seen significant demand increases.
mechanisms: GLP-1 receptor agonist that reduces appetite, slows gastric emptying, and improves insulin sensitivity for weight management.

evidence.summary: Thirty human studies including 27 randomized controlled trials demonstrated that semaglutide 2.4 mg produces clinically significant weight loss across multiple populations, with reductions of 14.9% versus 2.4% placebo in obesity, 15.8% versus 6.4% with liraglutide, and 9.6% versus 3.4% placebo in type 2 diabetes, while also resolving steatohepatitis without fibrosis worsening in 62.9% of patients versus 34.3% with placebo. Continued semaglutide treatment sustained weight loss at 7.9% below baseline compared to 6.9% weight gain after discontinuation.

keyStudies.authors: Perkovic V, Tuttle KR, Rossing P et al.

fdaStatus.notes: FDA Approved June 2021 FDA approved for type 2 diabetes (Ozempic) and weight management (Wegovy). Also approved for cardiovascular risk reduction.

name: CJC-1295 CJC-1295
shortDescription: is a growth hormone-releasing hormone (GHRH) analog available in two forms: with DAC (Drug Affinity Complex) for extended half-life, and without DAC (MOD GRF 1-29) for more pulsatile release.
longDescription: is a growth hormone-releasing hormone (GHRH) analog available in two forms: with DAC (Drug Affinity Complex) for extended half-life, and without DAC (MOD GRF 1-29) for more pulsatile release.
mechanisms: Stimulates the pituitary gland to release growth hormone by mimicking natural GHRH signaling. The DAC version provides sustained release while the non-DAC version allows more natural pulsatile patterns.

evidence.summary: Of 16 total studies identified, 7 were human studies including 3 randomized controlled trials. Key findings from human studies demonstrate that CJC-1295 produced sustained, dose-dependent increases in growth hormone and IGF-I levels while maintaining normal pulsatile GH secretion patterns and demonstrating a good safety profile.

name: IPAMORELIN
shortDescription: Ipamorelin is one of the most selective and cleanest growth hormone-releasing peptides, producing GH release without significant effects on cortisol or prolactin.
longDescription: Ipamorelin is one of the most selective and cleanest growth hormone-releasing peptides, producing GH release without significant effects on cortisol or prolactin. It's widely available through compounding pharmacies.
mechanisms: Selective ghrelin receptor agonist that stimulates clean GH release with minimal side effects and no impact on other hormones.

evidence.summary: Across 19 total studies (5 human, 2 RCTs), ipamorelin demonstrated dose-proportional pharmacokinetics with potent growth hormone stimulation and a 2-hour half-life, but showed no significant clinical benefit over placebo for postoperative ileus and lacks sufficient evidence for orthopedic applications. Safety data indicate good tolerability in human studies, though detection of ipamorelin and related GHRPs in black market products and varying urinary detection windows raise concerns about illicit use and analytical challenges.

name: MELANOTAN II
shortDescription: Melanotan II is a synthetic melanocortin peptide researched for its tanning and libido-enhancing effects.
longDescription: Melanotan II is a synthetic melanocortin peptide researched for its tanning and libido-enhancing effects. It stimulates melanin production and has notable sexual function effects.
mechanisms: Melanocortin receptor agonist that stimulates melanogenesis for tanning and activates pathways involved in sexual arousal.

evidence.summary: Clinical evidence for MELANOTAN II consists of 21 human studies including 3 randomized controlled trials. Key findings include efficacy for erectile dysfunction (85% response rate in affected men) and increased sexual desire, but also documented serious adverse events including renal infarction through thrombotic and toxic mechanisms and melanoma risk when combined with tanning bed use.

name: TESAMORELIN
shortDescription: Tesamorelin is a GHRH analog specifically researched and approved for reducing visceral adipose tissue.
longDescription: #### Visceral fat reduction with tesamorelin is associated with improved liver enzymes in HIV.
mechanisms: GHRH analog that stimulates natural GH release with particular efficacy for reducing abdominal/visceral fat.

evidence.summary: Across 30 human studies including 19 randomized controlled trials, tesamorelin has demonstrated FDA approval for reducing excess abdominal fat in HIV-infected patients with lipodystrophy, with consistent evidence showing significant reductions in visceral fat, hepatic fat, and trunk-to-appendicular fat ratio while producing minimal glycemic effects in HIV populations and reducing cholesterol without altering insulin response in type 2 diabetic patients.

name: MAZDUTIDE
shortDescription: Mazdutide is an emerging dual agonist peptide targeting both GLP-1 and glucagon receptors, representing the next generation of weight management research compounds.
longDescription: Mazdutide is an emerging dual agonist peptide targeting both GLP-1 and glucagon receptors, representing the next generation of weight management research compounds.
mechanisms: Dual GLP-1 and glucagon receptor agonist that may enhance metabolic effects through complementary pathways.

evidence.summary: Based on 25 total studies including 7 randomized controlled trials, mazdutide demonstrated dose-dependent weight loss ranging from 6.7% to 14% over 48 weeks compared to minimal weight loss or gain with placebo, and reduced HbA1c by 1.41-1.67% in type 2 diabetes patients. Among incretin analogs tested in phase 2-3 trials, retatrutide and tirzepatide showed superior efficacy for weight loss (up to 24.2%), with greater effects observed in patients with higher BMI and with longer treatment duration.

name: TB-500 TB-500

name: SERMORELIN
shortDescription: Sermorelin is a well-established GHRH analog that has been used for decades.
longDescription: Sermorelin is a well-established GHRH analog that has been used for decades. It's widely available through compounding pharmacies and considered one of the safest and most reliable growth hormone secretagogues.
mechanisms: GHRH (1-29) analog that stimulates natural, pulsatile GH release from the pituitary gland.

evidence.summary: The evidence base for sermorelin consists of 5 human studies with no randomized controlled trials, primarily focused on analytical detection methods and clinical perspectives rather than efficacy outcomes. Key findings include development of LC-MS/MS detection methods achieving sensitivity at or below 1 ng/mL in urine and capillary electrophoresis methods detecting sermorelin analogs at 75-200 ng/mL, along with a clinical perspective on its use for adult-onset growth hormone insufficiency management.

name: ARA-290 ARA-290
shortDescription: is a peptide derived from erythropoietin (EPO) researched for tissue repair and neuropathic pain applications without the blood-stimulating effects of EPO.
longDescription: is a peptide derived from erythropoietin (EPO) researched for tissue repair and neuropathic pain applications without the blood-stimulating effects of EPO.
mechanisms: Selectively activates the innate repair receptor (IRR) for tissue-protective effects without erythropoietic activity.

evidence.summary: Eight human studies including three randomized controlled trials demonstrate that ARA-290 reduces neuropathic pain symptoms and improves corneal nerve fiber density in patients with sarcoidosis and type 2 diabetes, with improvements also noted in quality of life measures and hemoglobin A1c levels. The mechanism appears to involve TRPV1 channel inhibition and modulation of immune-nociception pathways, with a safety profile favorable compared to placebo across the available clinical evidence.

shortDescription: Dermorphin is a naturally occurring opioid peptide originally isolated from frog skin, researched for its potent analgesic properties.
longDescription: # Dermorphin Clinical Evidence Summary Clinical evidence for dermorphin is limited to 11 human studies with no randomized controlled trials, though preclinical research across 30 total studies suggests the peptide produces potent analgesia with potentially reduced tolerance and dependence compared to morphine. Key findings indicate dermorphin functions as a high-efficacy opioid peptide analog with selective receptor binding properties, though human efficacy and safety data remain sparse.
mechanisms: Selective mu-opioid receptor agonist with significant analgesic potency.

name: DIHEXA
shortDescription: Dihexa is a potent nootropic peptide derived from angiotensin IV, researched for its remarkable ability to enhance cognitive function and promote neuroplasticity.
longDescription: Dihexa is a potent nootropic peptide derived from angiotensin IV, researched for its remarkable ability to enhance cognitive function and promote neuroplasticity. It is considered one of the most powerful cognitive-enhancing peptides studied.
mechanisms: Binds to hepatocyte growth factor (HGF) receptor, dramatically increasing its activity. This promotes synaptogenesis, neuronal survival, and neuroplasticity through the HGF/c-Met pathway.

evidence.summary: Of 9 total studies identified, only 4 involved human subjects and none were randomized controlled trials. Available evidence suggests DIHEXA may activate HGF/c-Met receptor pathways associated with memory and synaptic formation in Alzheimer's disease models, though clinical efficacy in humans remains unestablished due to the absence of RCT data.

name: SOMATROPIN (HGH)
shortDescription: Somatropin is recombinant human growth hormone (HGH) identical to naturally produced GH.
longDescription: # Clinical Evidence Summary for SOMATROPIN (HGH) Thirty human studies, including 10 randomized controlled trials, demonstrate that somatropin is safe and effective for promoting height gain in growth hormone deficiency and other growth disorders, with newer formulations such as once-weekly somatrogon and weekly lonapegsomatropin showing noninferior or superior efficacy to daily somatropin dosing in children. name: TRIPTORELIN
shortDescription: Triptorelin is a synthetic GnRH agonist used clinically for prostate cancer, endometriosis, and precocious puberty.
longDescription: Triptorelin is a synthetic GnRH agonist used clinically for prostate cancer, endometriosis, and precocious puberty. In research contexts, it is studied for hormonal axis reset and PCT protocols.
mechanisms: GnRH super-agonist that initially stimulates, then downregulates pituitary GnRH receptors, leading to suppressed and then restored gonadotropin release.

evidence.summary: Among 30 human studies (12 RCTs), triptorelin demonstrated efficacy in multiple indications: in advanced prostate cancer, a 6-month formulation maintained castrate testosterone levels in 93% of patients; in endometriosis, triptorelin improved pain symptoms and reduced endometriotic nodule volume; and in assisted reproduction for advanced-age women, dual GnRH agonist plus hCG triggering improved embryo quality and frozen embryo live birth rates compared to single GnRH agonist trigger.

name: VILON
shortDescription: Vilon is a synthetic dipeptide bioregulator researched for its potential effects on thymus function and immune aging.
longDescription: Vilon is a synthetic dipeptide bioregulator researched for its potential effects on thymus function and immune aging.
mechanisms: Short peptide that may influence thymic gene expression and immune cell development.

evidence.summary: Based on 20 total studies (6 human studies, 0 RCTs), VILON peptides demonstrated effects on immune function in laboratory and patient populations, including increased CD5 expression and T-cell differentiation in thymic cultures, promotion of thymocyte proliferation, heterochromatin reactivation in elderly lymphocytes, and improvements in coagulation, immune markers, and insulin requirements in elderly diabetic patients. However, the absence of randomized controlled trials limits the strength of evidence for clinical efficacy.
